questions.question: What is meant by public facilities?

options.A: Services provided for the welfare of all citizens

questions.explanation: Public facilities are services provided for the benefit of all people in society.

questions.question: Which institution is mainly responsible for providing public facilities?
questions.explanation: Governments are responsible for providing essential services to citizens.

questions.question: Which resource discussed in the chapter is considered an essential public facility?
questions.explanation: Clean drinking water is one of the most essential public facilities.

questions.question: Why are public facilities important in society?

options.A: They improve living conditions and public welfare

questions.explanation: Public facilities help people live healthier and better lives.

questions.question: Which organisation is responsible for water supply in many cities?
questions.explanation: Municipal corporations manage water supply in urban areas.

questions.question: What is one problem faced in providing public facilities?
questions.explanation: In many places people do not have equal access to public facilities.

questions.question: Which service ensures medical treatment for citizens?
questions.explanation: Public hospitals provide healthcare services.

questions.question: Which public facility is important for sanitation and hygiene?
questions.explanation: Clean water is essential for maintaining hygiene and sanitation.

questions.question: Which service helps people travel easily within cities?
questions.explanation: Public transport helps people travel at low cost.

questions.question: Which factor can limit access to public facilities?
questions.explanation: Economic inequality can limit access to essential services.

questions.question: Which facility provides education as a public service?
questions.explanation: Government schools provide education to the public.

questions.question: Which service maintains law and order in society?
questions.explanation: Police services ensure safety and law enforcement.

questions.question: Why must public facilities be accessible to all citizens?
questions.explanation: Public facilities must be available to everyone to promote equality.

questions.question: Which resource is necessary for cooking, drinking and cleaning?
questions.explanation: Water is essential for daily life activities.

questions.question: Which service supports communication between people?
questions.explanation: Postal services allow people to send letters and parcels.

questions.question: Which facility provides electricity to homes and industries?
questions.explanation: Electricity supply is an important public service.

questions.question: Which factor improves access to public facilities?
questions.explanation: Government policies help improve public services.

questions.question: Which public facility ensures cleanliness in cities?
questions.explanation: Sanitation services keep cities clean and hygienic.

questions.question: Which public service helps people travel long distances cheaply?
questions.explanation: Public transport systems provide affordable travel.

questions.question: Which issue arises when public facilities are not distributed fairly?
questions.explanation: Unequal access to services leads to inequality.

questions.question: Which facility helps improve literacy in society?
questions.explanation: Education improves literacy and knowledge.

questions.question: Which service ensures safety for citizens?
questions.explanation: Police services maintain law and order.

questions.question: Which factor helps improve public health?
questions.explanation: Clean water prevents diseases and improves health.

questions.question: Which facility is essential for modern communication?
questions.explanation: Postal services help in communication and delivery.

questions.question: Why are public facilities necessary in a democratic society?

options.A: They promote equality and public welfare

questions.explanation: Public facilities support citizens' welfare and equality.
